title: • Inevitable: Given everything that’s come before—his obse
content:
• Inevitable: Given everything that’s come before—his obsession, his need for validation, his rivalry with Fletcher—this moment couldn’t have ended any other way.

📌 McKee’s Principle Applied:
• Your graphic novel’s ending must feel unexpected in the moment, but perfect in hindsight.
• Ask: Has every character choice and conflict naturally led to this conclusion?

Activity: The Whiplash Story Problem Test

💡 Using Whiplash as a model, take a simple idea and develop it using McKee’s storytelling principles.
1. Write a one-sentence premise. (Example: A young fighter trains under a brutal coach to become a champion.)
2. Establish escalating conflict. (How does the coach push the fighter past their limits?)
3. Force decisions under pressure. (What sacrifices does the fighter make?)
4. Create a surprising yet inevitable climax. (Does the fighter win? Or does the real victory lie elsewhere?)
context: From document: 1.1.1. | The Story Problem | subheading — chunk 7 (chunk 7/8)
